items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits rise 2.8% in 2026
items.summary: The Social Security Administration says Social Security and SSI benefits will increase 2.8% in 2026. The increase starts with benefits payable in January 2026 for most Social Security beneficiaries, and SSI recipients begin receiving increased payments on December 31, 2025.

items.title: Medicaid.gov: Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026 adds multiple Medicaid/CHIP provisions
items.summary: CMS highlighted Medicaid and CHIP provisions in the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026 (P.L. 119-75), including a streamlined Medicaid/CHIP enrollment process for eligible out-of-state providers treating individuals under age 21 (effective 3 years after enactment, with a 5-year enrollment period). CMS also noted the law expands Medicaid Buy-In eligibility groups to include individuals age 65+ (states already offering the groups must cover 65+ by January 1, 2028).

items.title: CMS: Medicaid DSH cuts delayed until FY 2028 in CAA 2026
items.summary: CMS says the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026 delays implementation of Medicaid Disproportionate Share Hospital (DSH) cuts until fiscal year 2028. CMS also summarized a related provision that modifies the DSH payment adjustment formula to allow certain unreimbursed costs for patients dually eligible for Medicaid and other coverage to be included in the Medicaid shortfall calculation (effective upon enactment).

items.title: USDA WIC memo: FY 2026 fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts and April 2026 state requirement
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts for fruits and vegetables: $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA also notes WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits/vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: NEADA: LIHEAP funded at $4.045B for FY 2026 (up $20M)
items.summary: The National Energy Assistance Directors Association (NEADA) reports LIHEAP received $4.045 billion in FY 2026 funding, which it describes as a $20 million increase from FY 2025. NEADA also notes HHS released the remaining FY 2025 LIHEAP funds totaling $401.5 million on April 30, 2025 (states could draw down beginning May 1, 2025).

items.title: HUD PIH Notice 2025-28 highlights cost-saving options for Housing Choice Voucher agencies
items.summary: A NAHRO summary of HUD’s PIH Notice 2025-28 says public housing agencies can use multiple strategies to reduce Housing Assistance Payment (HAP) expenses to prevent shortfalls. Examples include reviewing payment standards to stay within 90%–110% of Fair Market Rent, adjusting utility allowances, reducing/stopping issuing turnover vouchers, and (with HUD approval in some cases) rescinding issued vouchers that have not yet resulted in an executed HAP contract.

items.title: IRS: 2025 individual tax returns due April 15, 2026
items.summary: The IRS states that calendar-year filers should file their 2025 federal individual income tax return by April 15, 2026. The IRS also notes you can request an automatic 6-month extension to file by submitting Form 4868 by the original due date, but an extension to file is not an extension to pay.

items.title: CMS snapshot: 76.8M enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP in Oct. 2025
items.summary: CMS reports that in October 2025, 76.8 million people were enrolled in Medicaid and CHIP (69.5 million in Medicaid and 7.2 million in CHIP). CMS also reports that between October 2024 and October 2025, Medicaid enrollment decreased by 2.6 million (4%), and CHIP enrollment decreased by 13,000 (less than 1%).

items.title: CMS announces new EPSDT behavioral health toolkit for states
items.summary: CMS said it published a new toolkit on Medicaid.gov on February 20, 2026: “State Medicaid & CHIP Toolkit for Children’s Behavioral Health Services and the Early and Periodic Screening, Diagnostic and Treatment (EPSDT) Requirements.” CMS describes it as a resource for state Medicaid and CHIP agencies to help children and youth with behavioral health conditions access needed care.
